target.title: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.
- Adds a before-tool-call fact adapter and wires it into the trusted-tool-policy import path so the adapter is called with real tool-call facts during policy evaluation.
- Removes the unsupported `security.matrix.audit.enabled` config gate, focused test, and docs reference.
- Removes all Security Matrix dead-code allowlist entries — the production files are now reachable through the trusted-tool-policy import graph.
- Hardens custom policy parsing so malformed decisions cannot weaken the default policy.

## Scope

This PR remains non-enforcing and observe-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution. The before-tool-call adapter is called during trusted-policy evaluation to build the audit event shape from explicit boundary facts, but the result is not emitted. Runtime diagnostic emission, canonical config, CLI display, and enforcement remain future work.

## Changes since last review

- Wired `createSecurityMatrixBeforeToolCallAuditEvent` into `src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts` — imported and called during `runTrustedToolPolicies` with real `toolName` from the event. The Security Matrix production files are now reachable through the runtime import graph.
- Removed all six Security Matrix files from both the required and optional Knip unused-file allowlists. The dead-code guard now sees them as used through the import path.
- Removed `SecurityMatrixBeforeToolCallAuditConfig` type and `isSecurityMatrixBeforeToolCallAuditEnabled` function from `before-tool-call.ts`.
- Deleted `src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts` (the config gate test).
- Removed docs sentence naming `security.matrix.audit.enabled`.

## Validation

Latest head: `523326d2f7`.

Focused commands run against this head:

```bash
# Unit tests: 27 evaluator + 45 capability + 3 default-policy + 3 before-tool-call = 78 total
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/security/security-matrix/tool-capability.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/default-policy.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/before-tool-call.test.ts

# Lint, docs, whitespace
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/ src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluation produces deterministic audit decisions from actor, influence source, capability, approval state, and operator policy. The before-tool-call adapter is wired into the trusted-tool-policy import path and builds the audit event from OpenClaw before-tool-call facts during policy evaluation. Malformed custom policy decisions are ignored, and custom policy overlays cannot weaken the default policy unless `allowPolicyWeakening` is explicitly set. Tool capability resolution handles OpenClaw-style namespaced tool IDs, file mutation verbs, credential/config/exec/git/browser/network capabilities, calendar writes, and email sends deterministically.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw development checkout in `/home/rev/projects/openclaw`; OpenClaw package version `2026.6.2`; branch `security/matrix-audit-model`; commit `523326d2f7ba8c40e73902b2ed71441b649f61de` (`523326d2f7`); Node.js `v24.15.0`; pnpm `11.2.2`; Linux `rev 6.19.14+kali-amd64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Kali 6.19.14-1+kali1 (2026-05-05) x86_64 GNU/Linux`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/tool-capability.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/default-policy.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/before-tool-call.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/tool-capability.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/default-policy.test.ts src/security/security-matrix/before-tool-call.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
   Start at  20:19:53
   Duration  224ms (transform 61ms, setup 0ms, import 81ms, tests 12ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw


 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  51 passed (51)
   Start at  20:19:56
   Duration  753ms (transform 620ms, setup 385ms, import 43ms, tests 137ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 15.11s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)
   Start at  20:20:16
   Duration  9.90s (transform 2.12s, setup 181ms, import 2.82s, tests 6.51s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 19.86s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The real command run against this checkout passed both Vitest shards for the Security Matrix test set: `1 passed (1)` with `27 passed (27)` evaluator tests and `3 passed (3)` with `51 passed (51)` capability/default-policy/before-tool-call tests. The config schema command also passed: `1 passed (1)` with `51 passed (51)`. Together this proves the evaluator, tool-capability mapping, default policy, before-tool-call adapter, and runtime config schema remain valid after the patch.

**What was not tested**: Unit tests exercise the evaluator, adapter, and capability mapping in isolation. A live OpenClaw tool call through the trusted-policy path was not exercised in this proof.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
